graphspace.title: Graph Space Management

load.unavailable: Unable to load the GraphSpace list.

graphspace.no_matches: No graph spaces match the current filters
graphspace.set_default_confirm: Confirm changing the default graph space?

form.name: Alias / Note
form.advanced_help: Only needed for Kubernetes, compute tasks, or quotas
form.k8s_placeholder: Start with a letter, lowercase letters/digits/hyphen, max 48 chars
form.id_help: The GraphSpace name is globally unique, is used in URLs, APIs, and authorization, and cannot be changed after creation. Example: demo_space
form.name_help: An optional display alias or note. It is not used for URL or authorization matching; when empty, the GraphSpace name is shown
form.auth_help: When enabled, access within this GraphSpace is checked by HugeGraph authorization. It cannot be changed after creation
form.max_graph_help: The maximum number of graphs allowed in this GraphSpace. Defaults to 100
form.graph_cpu_help: CPU limit for graph read and write services. Defaults to 64 vCPU
form.graph_memory_help: Memory limit for graph read and write services. Defaults to 128 GB
form.compute_cpu_help: CPU limit for asynchronous compute tasks. Defaults to 64 vCPU
form.compute_memory_help: Memory limit for asynchronous compute tasks. Defaults to 128 GB
form.oltp_namespace_help: Kubernetes namespace for graph read and write services. Leave blank outside Kubernetes
form.olap_namespace_help: Kubernetes namespace for asynchronous compute tasks. Leave blank when compute tasks are not used
form.storage_limit_help: Storage capacity limit for this GraphSpace. Defaults to 1000000 GB
form.image_format_error: Please enter a valid image address (e.g. example.com/org_1/xx_img:1.0.0)
form.id_rule: Start with a letter, lowercase letters/digits/underscore, max 48 chars
form.name_rule: Start with a letter or Chinese character; common symbols are allowed; max 48 characters
form.k8s_rule: Start with a letter, lowercase letters/digits/hyphen, max 48 chars

graph.title: Graph Management
graph.unavailable: Graphs are unavailable. Check the server connection and retry.

clear_confirm.title: Confirm graph clear
clear_confirm.scope: Deletion scope: graph schema and data. Back up both before clearing.
clear_confirm.input_label: Enter the graph name to confirm

sample.hlm_title: Build the Red Chamber demo graph?
sample.hlm_description: Target graph: {{graph}}. Adds the built-in Chinese literature dataset (14 characters and 15 relationships). It creates missing schema and elements only, never clears or updates matching data, and is safe to retry. Use a dedicated or empty graph; schema or ID conflicts stop the import without clearing existing data.
sample.loader_title: Build the People & Software demo graph?
sample.loader_description: Target graph: {{graph}}. Adds the people, software, knows, and created schema from the official HugeGraph Loader file example, plus 8 vertices and 6 edges. It never clears data or updates existing matching elements and is safe to retry. A schema or ID conflict stops the import and can be retried after correction.
sample.rank_description: Target graph: {{graph}}. Adds the official English Neighbor Rank example (14 vertices and 15 edges) for recommendation smoke tests. It never clears data and is retry-safe. Use a dedicated or empty graph because its person schema differs from the Loader example.
sample.failed: Example import failed. Existing data was not cleared; review the conflict and retry.

schema_view.load_failed: Schema could not be loaded. Check the Server connection and retry.
schema_view.export_failed: Schema export failed. Retry when the Server is available.

form.name: Graph Name
form.name_help: The graph name must be unique only within the current GraphSpace; graphs in different GraphSpaces can use the same name. It is used in URLs / APIs / authorization and cannot be changed after creation. Use lowercase letters, digits, and underscores, up to 48 characters
form.nickname_help: An optional display alias or note. It is not used for URL or authorization matching; when empty, the graph name is shown. Use letters, digits, or underscores, up to 48 characters
form.schema_load_failed: Schema templates could not be loaded.
form.schema_optional_hint: Optional. You can create an empty graph without a template
form.schema_create_failed: The built-in Graph Schema could not be created. Check the template list and retry.
form.schema_apply_failed: The graph was created, but its built-in Schema could not be applied. Retry from Metadata.
form.schema_verify_failed: The existing built-in Graph Schema could not be verified. Retry after the template service recovers.
form.schema_conflict: The existing {{name}} template differs from the built-in template. Rename it or use another template.

clone.unavailable: Clone is unavailable because the connected Server does not expose a compatible clone API.
clone.title: Clone Graph
clone.name: Cloned Graph Name
clone.name_duplicate: Use a graph name not already present in the destination GraphSpace
clone.nickname_duplicate: Enter a name different from the source graph

detail.creator: Creator
detail.update_success: Update requested. Refresh the page in a moment.

card.counts_tooltip: Actual vertex and edge counts reported by the graph list API
card.empty_schema_help: Model this graph or run a safe query
card.detail_tooltip: View the current vertex and edge counts for this graph

empty.description: This GraphSpace is ready but has no graphs yet. Create the first graph to model, import, and query data
empty.demo_prerequisite: After creating a graph, select it and use Data Import to build a quick Demo dataset.

form.id_help: The unique login identifier. It cannot be changed after creation
form.name: Display Name
form.name_help: The name shown to administrators. It can be changed later
form.is_superadmin_help: Super administrators can manage every GraphSpace and account. Grant this only to trusted users
form.remark_help: Optional context about the account purpose, team, or owner
form.default_password_help: The initial password for a new account. The user should change it after signing in
form.permission_help: Select the GraphSpaces this account may administer. HugeGraph roles control ordinary access

space_access.no_spaces: No GraphSpaces are available for this account.

task.metrics_failed: Could not load the import summary.

demo.title: Quick demo datasets
demo.target: Current target: {{graphspace}} / {{graph}}. These quick demos create missing schema and data without clearing the graph; they are separate from scheduled Loader tasks.
demo.choose_graph: Choose a target graph first, then return here to build a demo in a few seconds.

detail.title: Task Detail
detail.load_failed: Could not load task runs. Check the service and retry.

view.title: View Task
view.unavailable: Unable to load the current task details.
view.name: Task Name

edit.name: Task Name
edit.name_help: 1–48 Chinese characters, letters, numbers, or underscores. A number or underscore may be the first character
edit.workflow_title: Configure the import in four steps
edit.workflow_description: Choose a source and a graph that already has Schema, select source columns, map them to vertex or edge types, then choose when to run. You can go back without creating a task
edit.sync_type_help: Use Run Once for a one-off import, Scheduled for a source that supports recurring reads, or Realtime for Kafka
edit.cron_extra: Quartz order: second minute hour day month weekday year (optional). Every 5 minutes: 0 0/5 * * * ?
edit.source_help: The source controls file format or connection settings. Create and test it in Data Source Management first
edit.datasource_failed: Could not load the selected data source.
edit.graphspace_failed: Could not load the selected graph space.
edit.request_failed: Could not validate the task context.
edit.load_graphs_failed: Could not load graphs for the selected graph space.
edit.load_fields_failed: Could not load fields from the selected data source.
edit.submit_failed: Could not create the import task. Check the service and retry.
edit.graphspace_full: Graph space {{graphspace}} has reached its storage limit and cannot accept more data.
edit.duplicate_check_failed: Failed to check duplicate task name
edit.name_rule: Use Chinese characters, letters, numbers, or underscores, up to 48 characters
edit.target_help: Data is written only to the selected graph. This task does not clear the graph; duplicate handling follows the mapping and schema ID strategy
edit.loader_docs_intro: For data formats and advanced Loader options, see the
edit.demo_title: Target graph, schema, and demo data
edit.demo_target: Current target: {{graphspace}} / {{graph}}. Prepare the demo schema and data here, then continue configuring the import task.
edit.demo_choose_graph: Choose a target graph first. Empty-schema graphs are available so a demo can prepare both schema and data.
edit.demo_failed: Could not prepare the demo schema and data.
edit.prepare_schema_first: This graph has no schema. Build a demo first or create schema on the Graph Schema page
edit.fields_help_title: Select only columns used by IDs or properties
edit.fields_help: Detected columns appear on the left. Add a missing header only when the source has no usable header; its spelling must match the incoming data
edit.add_field_placeholder: Add field: letters, numbers, hyphen, and underscore only
edit.mapping_required_tip: Create at least one vertex or edge mapping
edit.mapping_help_title: Map source columns to the existing graph Schema
edit.mapping_help: A vertex ID column is required only for custom ID strategies. Edge source and target IDs must resolve to the endpoint vertex IDs. Auto Match only pairs identical column and property names; review it before continuing
edit.mapping_example_title: Complete example (select each value in the mapping drawer)
edit.mapping_example_vertex_source: Vertex source row: person_id=42, person_name=Alice, status=A
edit.mapping_example_vertex_target: Vertex mapping: label=person; ID column=person_id; property person_name → name; value status:A → active
edit.mapping_example_edge_source: Edge source row: from_id=42, to_id=84, since_year=2024
edit.mapping_example_edge_target: Edge mapping: label=knows; source ID=from_id; target ID=to_id; property since_year → since
edit.mapping_meta_failed: The target Schema could not be loaded, so mappings cannot be configured safely.
edit.vertex_type_help: Choose an existing vertex label from the target graph Schema. Each source row creates or updates a vertex of this type
edit.edge_type_help: Choose an existing edge label from the target graph Schema. Its endpoint types must match the Schema definition
edit.id_column_help: Current vertex ID strategy: {{strategy}}. PRIMARY_KEY and AUTOMATIC derive IDs without a separate ID column; CUSTOMIZE_* strategies require one
edit.edge_id_help: Choose the source column containing the endpoint vertex ID, not the edge ID
edit.property_mapping_help: Map incoming columns to Schema properties. Unmapped columns are ignored
edit.value_mapping_help: Optional: replace specific incoming values before writing them
edit.select_id_field: Select the source field containing the vertex ID
edit.incomplete_property_mapping: Select both a source field and Schema property, or remove the empty row
edit.incomplete_value_mapping: Enter the source field, original value, and replacement value, or remove the empty row
edit.schedule_help_title: Choose when this task should read the source
edit.schedule_help: Run Once is the safest default for files and initial imports. Scheduled uses a Quartz expression. Kafka uses realtime consumption
edit.load_type_help: Full reads the current source. Use incremental only when the source and deployment explicitly support new-record consumption

datasource.title: Data Sources
datasource.delete_content: This data source will be removed from the system. Delete this data source?
datasource.load_failed: Could not load data sources. Check the service and retry.
datasource.empty_description: Add a data source before creating an import task

form.template_help: Fills an editable safe example without connecting to a service or uploading a file
form.field_help: The meaning and format of {{label}} depend on the selected data source type. See the examples and Loader documentation in this form
form.name: Data Source Name
form.name_help: Unique name for this data source in the current GraphSpace. Use Chinese characters, letters, numbers, or underscores only
form.name_rule: Use Chinese characters, letters, numbers, or underscores only
form.type_help: Select where the data resides. Changing the type clears fields that belong only to the previous type
form.auth_type_help: Use None for ordinary clusters. Select Kerberos only when the cluster requires it and provide its matching configuration
form.keytab_file_help: Upload the Kerberos keytab that matches the configured principal
form.conf_file_help: Upload the krb5.conf needed by the client to locate and access the KDC
form.principal_help: Kerberos identity, usually user@REALM. It must match the uploaded keytab
form.krb5_conf_help: krb5.conf used by the Hive Kerberos client
form.user_name_help: Hadoop user name used by the Hive Kerberos connection
form.user_keytab_help: Upload the keytab that matches the Hive user principal
form.zk_quorum_help: ZooKeeper endpoints used for Hive service discovery, for example zk-1:2181,zk-2:2181
form.file_type_help: CSV uses commas, TEXT supports a custom column delimiter, and JSON reads object fields
form.header_help: Optional comma-separated names for files without a header row, for example: id,name,age
form.hdfs_path_help: Path to the source file or directory in the configured HDFS cluster
form.core_site_help: Upload the Hadoop core-site.xml used to resolve the HDFS endpoint
form.hdfs_site_help: Optional. Upload this cluster's hdfs-site.xml only when overriding the HDFS client defaults. XML files only
form.loader_docs: View HugeGraph Loader data-source and field documentation
form.delimiter_help: Single-character separator between TEXT columns. CSV always uses a comma
form.charset_help: Character encoding used by the source data. A mismatch causes garbled text or parsing failures
form.date_format_help: Java date pattern used for date properties, for example yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss
form.time_zone_help: GMT zone used for date values without a zone, from GMT-12 through GMT+12
form.skipped_line_help: Regular expression for lines to ignore. The default skips lines beginning with # or //
form.compression_help: Must match the source file's actual compression or columnar format. Use NONE for an ordinary file
form.local_file_help: Upload one file matching the selected format and compression. Column order must match the later mapping
form.kafka_topic_help: Name of the existing topic that Loader will continuously consume
form.from_beginning_help: Without a committed offset, start at the earliest available offset when enabled or the latest when disabled. Existing group offsets still take precedence
form.db_type_help: Select the vendor to fill Loader's supported JDBC driver and apply the correct schema rules
form.jdbc_driver_help: JDBC driver class filled from the selected vendor. The corresponding driver must exist in the runtime
form.jdbc_url_help: Use the JDBC URL for the selected database. The connection test also checks credentials and the target table
form.database_schema_help: Optional for PostgreSQL and Oracle, required for SQL Server, and normally unused for MySQL
form.url_rule: Enter a valid JDBC URL, for example: jdbc:mysql://127.0.0.1:3306/db_name
form.database_help: Database or catalog that Loader will read through this JDBC connection
form.table_help: Existing table Loader will read. The connection test checks that it is accessible
form.username_help: JDBC account with read access to the target database and table
form.password_help: Password for the JDBC account. Templates never prefill it; the backend stores it with the data-source configuration after submission
form.where_help: Filter expression after WHERE, without the WHERE keyword. Leave empty to read the whole table
form.connection_unsupported: JDBC datasource check is not supported locally

schema.delete_failed: Delete failed. Check the server connection and retry.

schema.name_placeholder: Letters, numbers, underscores, and Chinese characters are allowed
schema.delete_irreversible: This action cannot be undone. Please proceed with caution.
schema.delete_task_hint: Deleting metadata may take a while. Check Async Tasks for progress

common.delete_warning: This action cannot be undone. Please proceed with caution.
common.name_placeholder: Letters, numbers, underscores, and Chinese characters are allowed

property.in_use: Properties {{names}} are in use and cannot be deleted.

form.name: Property Name
form.name_help: The property key used when reading or writing vertices and edges. Examples: created_at and age. It cannot be changed after creation
form.type_help: How values are stored. Use TEXT for text, INT/LONG for integers, and DATE for date-time values. It must match imported data
form.cardinality_help: Values allowed per element: SINGLE stores one value, LIST stores ordered values with duplicates, and SET stores unique values

vertex.detail_failed: Vertex type details could not be loaded. Retry before editing.
vertex.in_use: Vertices {{names}} are in use and cannot be deleted.
vertex.delete_warning: This action cannot be undone. Please proceed with caution.
vertex.delete_task_note: Deleting metadata may take a while. Check Async Tasks for progress.

edge.detail_failed: Edge type details could not be loaded. Retry before editing.
edge.delete_warning: This action cannot be undone. Please proceed with caution.
edge.delete_task_note: Deleting metadata may take a while. Check Async Tasks for progress.

image_view.load_failed: Schema diagram data could not be loaded. Retry before editing.
image_view.empty_description: Define reusable properties first, then create vertex and edge types
image_view.template_description: Choose a built-in example or a saved template from this GraphSpace and apply it to the current graph
image_view.template_placeholder: Select a Schema template to apply
image_view.template_picker_help: The template is applied to this graph without deleting or replacing anything on the Schema Template Management page
image_view.no_saved_templates: This GraphSpace has no user templates yet. Built-in examples are still available
image_view.template_apply_failed: Could not fully apply the Schema template. The Server may have created part of it; the current graph has been refreshed for review
image_view.template_load_failed: Could not load Schema templates. Check the service and retry
image_view.edge_prerequisite: Create at least one vertex type before creating an edge type.
image_view.start_description: This graph has no schema yet. Start by defining a reusable property

schema_template.load_failed: Could not load schema templates. Check the service and retry.

read_only.title: Read-only template library
read_only.description: You can browse built-in and saved templates here. Creating, saving, editing, and deleting templates requires PD mode
read_only.choose_graph: Choose a graph to apply a template

schema_template.create_duplicate: A schema template named {{name}} already exists. Choose another name.
schema_template.create_failed: Could not create the schema template. Review the input and retry.
schema_template.update_failed: Could not update the schema template. Review the input and retry.

builtin_description.people_network: A single-primary-key example with person vertices, relationship edges, and a secondary index
builtin_description.product_catalog: A composite-primary-key example with product vertices, relationship edges, and a range index

builtin_section.title: Example Templates
builtin_section.description: These examples ship with Hubble and are not saved on the Server. Review or change one first; it becomes an editable, deletable user template only after a successful save

user_section.title: User Templates
user_section.description: These templates come from the Server. Select a row to expand its complete Groovy Schema

docs.intro: Need guidance on property graphs, vertex ID strategies, and Schema modeling?

form.name: Template Name
form.name_help: Identifies this reusable template in the current GraphSpace, for example movie_network
form.schema_help: Define properties, vertices, edges, then indexes. Prefer ifNotExist() so the template is safe to reuse
form.starting_point_help: Available only while the draft is empty. Review and edit the example before submitting; selection never creates or overwrites graph data

operations.dashboard_external_context: This is an optional external monitoring entry. Being unconfigured or unavailable does not affect Hubble's native Cluster Overview or Nodes features.
operations.overview_description: Understand cluster topology, service tiers and node status at a glance
operations.nodes_description: Browse, filter and inspect every discovered service node
operations.node_identity_help: Service-reported node name {{name}}. Full node ID: {{id}}
operations.node_id_copy_failed: Could not copy the node ID
operations.load_failed: Unable to load current cluster data
operations.refresh_failed: Refresh failed. The last successful snapshot remains visible.
operations.logical_relationship: Logical service relationship, not network traffic
operations.topology_label: Server, PD, and Store service topology
operations.health_only_description: Node identities, topology, capacity, disk, and Raft details are restricted
operations.empty_cluster: No nodes were discovered. Check the trusted Hubble service configuration.
operations.node_unavailable: This node is unavailable or no longer belongs to the authorized snapshot.
operations.metric_scope_store_only: {{metric}} metrics are collected from Store nodes, not {{nodeType}} nodes.
operations.metric_scope_backend: Backend metrics are provided by Server and Store nodes, not {{nodeType}} nodes.
operations.reason_partial_refresh_failed: Some metrics could not be refreshed
operations.reason_deployment_mode_unsupported: Unsupported by the current deployment mode

navigation_page.dashboard_unavailable: Dashboard is unavailable. Check dashboard.address and service health.
